dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9483 IH: Clean Ports for Commerce Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scholten introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To require the Corps of Engineers to carry out advanced planning for dredging activities in areas with known PFAS contamination, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Clean Ports for Commerce Act.

subsection.text: Prior to carrying out any dredging activities in an area with respect to which the Secretary has information indicating that the dredged materials will contain perfluoroalkyl or polyfluoroalkyl substances, the Secretary shall identify—

paragraph.text: the disposal site for the dredged materials;
how the Secretary will mitigate the effects of the dredging activities on water quality and public health with respect to such substances; and
any potential delays in carrying out the activities related to the presence of such substances.

subsection.text: In this section, the term Secretary means the Secretary of the Army, acting through the Chief of Engineers.
